# services.cyrus-imap

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.cyrus-imap`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.cyrus-imap.cyrusConfigFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path to the configuration file used for Cyrus. |
| `services.cyrus-imap.cyrusSettings` | `open submodule of attribute set of attribute set of (boolean or signed integer or list of string)` | Cyrus configuration settings. See [cyrus.conf(5)](https://www.cyrusimap.org/imap/reference/manpages/configs/cyrus.conf.html) |
| `services.cyrus-imap.cyrusSettings.DAEMON` | `unspecified value` | This section lists long running daemons to start before any SERVICES are spawned. {manpage}`master(8)` will ensure that these processes are running, restarting any process which dies or forks. All listed processes will be shutdown when {manpage}`master(8)` is exiting. |
| `services.cyrus-imap.cyrusSettings.EVENTS` | `unspecified value` | This section lists processes that should be run at specific intervals, similar to cron jobs. This section is typically used to perform scheduled cleanup/maintenance. |
| `services.cyrus-imap.cyrusSettings.SERVICES` | `unspecified value` | This section is the heart of the cyrus.conf file. It lists the processes that should be spawned to handle client connections made on certain Internet/UNIX sockets. |
| `services.cyrus-imap.cyrusSettings.START` | `unspecified value` | This section lists the processes to run before any SERVICES are spawned. This section is typically used to initialize databases. Master itself will not startup until all tasks in START have completed, so put no blocking commands here. |
| `services.cyrus-imap.debug` | `boolean` | Whether to enable debugging messages for the Cyrus master process. |
| `services.cyrus-imap.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Cyrus IMAP, an email, contacts and calendar server. |
| `services.cyrus-imap.group` | `null or string` | Cyrus IMAP group name. If this is not set, a group named `cyrus` will be created. |
| `services.cyrus-imap.imapdConfigFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path to the configuration file used for cyrus-imap. |
| `services.cyrus-imap.imapdSettings` | `open submodule of attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean or list of string)` | IMAP configuration settings. See [imapd.conf(5)](https://www.cyrusimap.org/imap/reference/manpages/configs/imapd.conf.html) |
| `services.cyrus-imap.imapdSettings.configdirectory` | `absolute path` | The pathname of the IMAP configuration directory. |
| `services.cyrus-imap.imapdSettings.idlesocket` | `absolute path` | Unix socket that idled listens on. |
| `services.cyrus-imap.imapdSettings.lmtpsocket` | `absolute path` | Unix socket that lmtpd listens on, used by {manpage}`deliver(8)`. This should match the path specified in {manpage}`cyrus.conf(5)`. |
| `services.cyrus-imap.imapdSettings.notifysocket` | `absolute path` | Unix domain socket that the mail notification daemon listens on. |
| `services.cyrus-imap.listenQueue` | `signed integer` | Socket listen queue backlog size. See {manpage}`listen(2)` for more information about a backlog. Default is 32, which may be increased if you have a very high connection rate. |
| `services.cyrus-imap.tmpDBDir` | `absolute path` | Location where DB files are stored. Databases in this directory are recreated upon startup, so ideally they should live in ephemeral storage for best performance. |
| `services.cyrus-imap.user` | `null or string` | Cyrus IMAP user name. If this is not set, a user named `cyrus` will be created. |
